action: Open the Start menu by clicking the Windows icon at the bottom left of the screen. Then type 'Notepad' in the search bar, open the Notepad application, and type 'hi sathish' in the Notepad window.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: is the first action. The task requires opening the Start menu by clicking the Windows icon at the bottom left of the screen. The Windows icon is typically located at the bottom left and is represented by Box ID 151. I will click this icon to open the Start menu.

previous action: The previous step was to click the Windows icon to open the Start menu. On the current screen, the Start menu is now visible, with a search bar labeled 'Search for apps; settings; and documents' (Box ID 11) at the top. This confirms the Start menu is open. The next step is to type 'Notepad' in the search bar to search for the Notepad application.

previous action: The previous step was to type 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar. On the current screen, the search results are visible, and the Notepad application icon is present (Box ID 123, labeled 'Notepad'). This confirms that the search for Notepad was successful. The next step is to open the Notepad application by clicking on its icon.

previous action: The previous step was to click the Notepad icon in the Start menu. On the current screen, the Notepad application window is now open and visible. The main text editing area is present, which is the largest central region of the Notepad window. The next step is to type 'hi sathish' into the Notepad window. Since the text area is already focused by default when Notepad opens, I can proceed to type the required text.
